container.title: Rules
container.description:
In choosing units you must ensure that you complete no more than 10 Level one units (60 credit points), and at least six units (36 credit points) at Level 3 of which at least four (24 credit points) are Bachelor of Information Technology listed (FIT-coded) units.

Note: If you are in a double degree with the Bachelor of Information Technology, no more than 36 credit points can be completed at Level 1.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure: 2026.03.008

container.title: Part C. Free elective studies
Discipline elective studies
container.description:
To enhance your IT studies the Faculty recommends the following minors and elective units which can be taken as part of your free electives. Other IT minors are also available, check the Faculty Handbook pages.

Note: If you are doing the Applied cybersecurity major you cannot do the Cybersecurity minor.

Curriculum Structure Container: Part C. Free elective studies

container.description:
Note 1: Your elective units may be chosen from any of the units offered at Monash to suit your interests or career aspirations. You may choose to take other units available in the Bachelor of Information Technology course to extend your major or to complete a second major or minor(s) from the course, or you may take Faculty of IT units from C2001 Bachelor of Computer Science, or the software engineering specialisation in the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours).

Elective units may also be used to sample from across the University or to complete a major or minor(s) from another course as long as you have completed the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units. The units may be at any level, however, no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 may be credited to the Bachelor of Information Technology and a minimum 36 credit points must be at Level 3.

Note 2: If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the partner degree are credited as electives towards this degree.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure: 2026.03.008

container.title: Part A. Specified studies
Professional placement
container.description:
FIT3045 is only available if you are selected to participate in the Industry Based Learning placement program. [https://www.monash.edu/it/industry-based-learning]

If you are selected for the IBL program you will complete FIT3045 Industry-based learning (18 credit points) and FIT2108 Industry-based learning (0 points); or FIT3045 (18 credit points), FIT2108 (0 points) and either FIT3201 Industry-based learning onboarding (8 weeks) (0 points) or FIT3202 Industry-based learning onboarding (4 weeks) (0 points) (depending on the duration of your placement). The IBL placement will replace your Applied studies project units and the nominated Level 3 unit in your major.

In the IBL program you must complete a unit over summer semester or overload in one semester by one unit in order to complete the degree within three years (this is because you only complete 18 credit points of credit during the IBL placement semester). You must have completed at least three semesters of your course before starting your IBL placement.

container.title: Part B. Major studies
container.description:
You must complete at least one of the following Information Technology majors.

A major requires eight units with no more than two units at Level one (12 credit points) and at least three units (18 credit points) at Level three.
